# govulncheck CVE allowlist (KM-178)
#
# govulncheck has no native ignore/suppress mechanism. This file is the
# relief valve: the cve-gate workflow's govulncheck job runs `govulncheck
# -json ./...`, extracts every reachable finding's OSV id, and fails ONLY on
# findings whose id is NOT listed here.
#
# WHY THIS EXISTS
# govulncheck pulls a fresh vulnerability DB on every run (vuln.go.dev updates
# daily). A newly-published CVE in a dependency you have not touched can fail
# an otherwise-unrelated PR. That is the gate working as designed — but it can
# hard-block work that has nothing to do with the new advisory. When that
# happens, the operator has two choices:
#   1. (Preferred) Fix the finding — bump the dep / Go toolchain that clears it.
#   2. (Relief valve) Add the OSV/GO id here with a tracked justification, so
#      the gate stops blocking while a follow-up card handles the real fix.
#
# RULES
#   - One id per line. Format: the govulncheck OSV id, e.g. GO-2026-4971.
#   - Blank lines and lines starting with '#' are ignored.
#   - Inline trailing comments are NOT supported — put justification on the
#     comment line ABOVE the id (see format below).
#   - EVERY entry MUST be justified. An unjustified suppression is a silent
#     security hole; reviewers (Leon) reject allowlist additions without a
#     reason + tracking card.
#
# FORMAT (copy this block per entry):
#   # <date> <who> — <one-line reason> — tracked in KM-XXX
#   GO-YYYY-NNNN
#
# An empty allowlist (this file with only comments) means: fail on ANY
# reachable finding. That is the intended steady state. Entries here are
# temporary by definition — each should reference a card that removes it.
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# (no active suppressions — steady state: the gate fails on any finding)
